entries.direction_title: Baseline: no new SEN policy, observe passive trajectory
entries.rationale: T1 explicitly constructs the passive, no-new-policy counterfactual describing drift in identification, capacity, and inequality under current rules, without proposing intervention, directly fulfilling S1's bounded scope.

entries.direction_title: Verify and reconcile the identification trend and its drivers
entries.rationale: T2 is dedicated to reconciling inconsistent statistical series and decomposing prevalence, diagnostic-practice, access, and incentive drivers, plus testing county variation, matching S2's evidence-and-monitoring-architecture boundary exactly.

T3 addresses SNI/BTMN category boundaries, expert-committee gatekeeping restructuring, and waiting lists, explicitly using transferability-tested comparative tiered/non-categorical models (Wales, Finland), matching S3's definitional-and-diagnostic-structure boundary.

entries.direction_title: Realign finance incentives away from diagnosis-gated funding
entries.rationale: T4 focuses on redesigning per-pupil weighting and normative multipliers toward census/needs-based allocation as an incentive-design mechanism, matching S4's funding-design boundary and explicitly excluding service-delivery capacity.

entries.direction_title: Build specialist and mainstream support capacity
entries.rationale: T5 targets gyógypedagógus shortages, regional maldistribution, traveling networks, and the assess-to-serve implementation gap, matching S5's workforce-and-support-capacity boundary while excluding identification rules and finance formulas.

entries.direction_title: Address sorting, school choice, and segregation interaction
entries.rationale: T6's anti-segregation and school-choice component directly confronts how SEN identification interacts with free choice, institutional selectivity, and Roma over-classification to reshape composition, including outstanding ECtHR/EU legal-accountability obligations, matching S6.

entries.direction_title: Coordinate adjacent-domain policy levers affecting identification
entries.rationale: T6's cross-domain coordination mechanism explicitly addresses school-readiness/entry-age rules, rural network structure, and private/church school growth as adjacent-domain levers measurably affecting SEN identification and capacity, matching S7's coordination boundary.
